questions.question: Who were driven out of their homeland during the process of creation of Zamins under Permanent Settlement?

(i) The Company received 22.5 million from Mir Jafar and invested it to propel the industrial revolution in Britain.
options: (ii) Kols organized an insurrection in 1831-1832, which was directed against government officers and moneylenders.
(iii) In 1855, two Santhal brothers, Sidhu and Kanu, led the Santhal Rebellion.
(iv) In 1879, an Act was passed to regulate the territories occupied by the Santhals.
questions.explanation: Book-back answer: i), ii) and iii) are correct

questions.question: Assersion (A) : The Revolt of 1857 was brutally suppressed by the British army.
options: Reason (R) : The failure of the rebellion was due to the absence of Central authority.
Both (A) and (R) are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
questions.explanation: Book-back answer: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)

questions.question: The Constitutional Head of the Union is ________.

i) The Chief Justice and other judges of the Supreme Court hold the office up to the age of 62 years.
options: ii) Judiciary is the third organ of the government.
iii) The cases involving fundamental rights come under the Appellate jurisdiction of the Supreme Court.
iv) The law declared by Supreme Court is binding on all courts within the territory of India.
questions.explanation: Book-back answer: ii & iv are correct

options: Reason(R) : The Himalayas prevents cold winds from central Asia and keep the Indian Sub-continent warm.
Both (A) and (R) are true: R explains A
Both (A) and (R) are true: R does not explain A
questions.explanation: Book-back answer: Both (A) and (R) are true; R explains A

questions.question: Tidal forests are found in and around ________.
